title: Media Delivery, Range Requests, MP4, HLS, and Streaming Boundaries · Language Lens: Cloudfront Range Behavior
summary: A video player downloads whole large files for seeking, an origin emits invalid range responses, MP4 metadata sits at the end, and private segment access differs from manifest access. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cloudfront range behavior increment in accessible media-delivery and range plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Meet ascending, nonoverlapping, valid Range behavior, understand larger origin fetches, 200 fallbacks, chunked-origin limits, and cache chunking.
Configure Brotli or gzip variants, Accept-Encoding cache behavior, already-compressed media, content length, and changed client capability evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Cloudfront Range Behavior · Theory Cloudfront Range Behavior
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 4446 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate CloudFront range shape, progressive media layout, and accessible playback; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Meet ascending, nonoverlapping, valid Range behavior, understand larger origin fetches, 200 fallbacks, chunked-origin limits, and cache chunking.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cloudfront Range Behavior contract for the supplied data.
